project.title: Identification of RNF187 binding proteins by LC-MS/MS
project.projectDescription: To explore the regulatory network involving RNF187 binding proteins, immunocoprecipitation (IP) experiments were performed in human spermatogonial stem cells (SSCs) via LC-MS/MS analysis and Label-free quantification.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Human SSCs were cultured in Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium and nutrition Mixture F-12 (DMEM/F-12; Gibco, USA), supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS; ScienCell, USA) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin solution (NCM Biotech, China). Human SSCs were transfected with GFP and Flag-RNF187 plasmids, and immunoprecipitation was performed using an antibody against the exogenous Flag tag on the lysates of transfected SSCs. The bound protein was eluted by heating the beads at 95 °C for 5 min with 2× SDS buffer and then separated by SDS-PAGE. The gels were first divided into small 1mm³ particles and then sequentially rinsed with water, 50% ACN, and 100% ACN. Following this, the gel particles were treated with DTT for reduction, IAA for alkylation, and trypsin for digestion, which was carried out at 37°C over a period of 12 hours. The digestion was terminated by 0.1% TFA, and the resulting peptides were extracted from the gel particles. These peptides were subsequently desalted using StageTip (Thermofisher) in preparation for mass spectrometry analysis. For the mass spectrometry analysis, the purified peptides were reconstituted in a solution containing 0.1% formic acid (FA) and then separated using an analytical column (75 μm × 160 mm, 1.9 μm, Dr. Maisch). The separation was performed on an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos mass spectrometer (Thermofisher) integrated with an Easy-nLC 1200 system (Thermofisher). The instrument operated in data-dependent acquisition (DDA) mode, with MS1 spectra collected in the Orbitrap across a mass range of 350-1500 at a resolution of 60k. For MS/MS analysis, high-energy collision dissociation (HCD) was utilized, and the spectra were acquired in the Orbitrap at a resolution of 15k.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The raw data were analyzed using MaxQuant software (version 1.6.5.0), with searches conducted against the Human database (retrieved in May 2023). The search parameters included Trypsin/P as the enzyme, permitting up to two missed cleavages. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues (+57.0215 Da) was designated as a fixed modification, while variable modifications accounted for methionine oxidation and N-terminal acetylation of proteins. Peptides and proteins were identified with a false discovery rate (FDR) cutoff of 1%.
